page: Microsoft Azure M64m is a memory-intensive server instance powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor. This configuration delivers 64 vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 38 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

Designed for memory-heavy workloads, the M64m provides 1792.0 GB of system memory, translating to a high density of 28.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It also includes 7340 GB of local storage. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance, and it does not feature any GPU acceleration.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. However, multi-core and parallelized execution is significantly stronger; the bzip3 multi-threaded decompression benchmark ranks in the top 25% with 188.37 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth remains steady in the average tier, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 2438.65 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 73.3 GB/sec.

Given its massive memory capacity and substantial local storage, the M64m offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high resource density rather than raw single-threaded compute speed. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis (which achieved 39.98 million SET operations per second in benchmarks), enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure M64m is a memory-intensive server instance configured with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores on a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 1792.0 GB of RAM, yielding 28.0 GB of memory per core, alongside 7340 GB of local storage. The instance lacks GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core metrics are weak, but multi-threaded capabilities are robust, as evidenced by a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 188.37 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, with Redis SET operations reaching 39.98 million per second. This profile makes the M64m qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise applications, large-scale databases, and in-memory data processing rather than compute-heavy or single-threaded workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64m is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 1792 GB of RAM, and 7340 GB of local storage, designed for large-scale databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64m is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1792 GB RAM, and 7340 GB local storage, built for large database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM capacity and local storage for demanding enterprise database and in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor
1792.0 GB of RAM providing a high density of 28.0 GB per core
7340 GB of local storage bundled without GPU acceleration
Strong multi-threaded performance balanced by weaker single-core execution
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and in-memory data processing
